title: Raqqa — The Fall
introText:
October 2017. Raqqa fell last week.
You've driven this one six times before. He knows your sister's name.
Camera. Recorder. Years of notebooks in a leather satchel. A thermos.
The SDF is running guided press tours. He doesn't want the tour -- he wants the prison.
Engine on at 06:00. The crossing into Rojava is one of your back routes.

beats.text:
Children selling cigarettes on the main road. The buildings behind them have no roofs.
The prison compound is two kilometres past the last SDF checkpoint.

endings.text:
The prison warden lets him in for forty minutes. Three interviews. Two on camera.
Eleven thousand words. Two issues. The following spring.
He sends you a copy. Your name is in the acknowledgments, spelled wrong.

The warden changes his mind at the gate. No cameras. No recorder. Notebooks only.
He writes for an hour. Eleven thousand words sit on his hard drive for three months.
A twelve-minute podcast episode airs in December. The detainees are not named.
